subject: fix(edges): context-aware update toast + document dark-mode contract
body:
Two items from the `edges branch` trigger:

1. handleCheckForUpdates 'update-available' toast now branches on
   isInstalled. The header Update button only renders when
   `Platform.OS === 'web' && isInstalled && hasUpdate` (home.tsx:620),
   so a non-installed web user (browser-tab, not installed PWA) who
   triggers the manual check and gets `update-available` would
   previously see "tap Update in the header" — but the header has no
   such button to tap. Now:
     - isInstalled: "An update is available — tap Update in the header"
     - not installed: "An update is available — refresh the page to apply"
   Network-first SW caching means a hard reload reliably picks up the
   new bundle for browser users, so refresh is the correct affordance.
   Updated docs/TODO.md verification scenario #7 to cover both branches.

2. Documented the storage contract for `synctone_dark_mode` in the
   theme flash-prevention inline script comment. Only three valid
   states: absent, 'true', 'false'. Any other value silently maps to
   light mode because the comparison is `darkRaw === 'true'`. The app
   only ever writes 'true'/'false' via AsyncStorage's web backend, so
   the silent fallback is operationally safe — but if a future caller
   writes a different shape (e.g. 'TRUE' from a refactor or a
   third-party migration tool), the silent-light-fallback would mask a
   real bug. Comment instructs maintainers to fix the writer rather
   than relax the check.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed

Tags: bugfix, ux, pwa, docs
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch
